addressedReviewFindings.finding: Numeric prompt rendering normalized 1.0e-6 to 1e-6, diverging from TypeScript/Bun String(number) and JSON.stringify fixed-decimal output 0.000001 at the lower decimal threshold.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Prompt number formatting now expands negative exponents for finite values with abs(value) >= 1.0e-6 and < 1.0e21 while preserving exponential output for 1.0e-7. Scalar and nested prompt JSON tests now cover 1.0e-6. JavaScript reference command, focused Swift tests, and full Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 209 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Recursive prompt JSON rendering used default JSONEncoder string escaping, so nested array/object string values escaped forward slashes unlike TypeScript/Bun JSON.stringify and the prior .withoutEscapingSlashes compactJSONString contract.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Prompt JSON string rendering now uses .withoutEscapingSlashes while preserving recursive TypeScript-compatible number formatting. The nested prompt JSON regression now covers 1.0e20 plus URL-like strings in objects and arrays. JavaScript reference command, focused Swift test, and full Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 209 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Array/object prompt rendering still used JSONEncoder compact JSON for nested numbers, so nested large numbers rendered as 1e+20 instead of TypeScript/Bun JSON.stringify decimal output.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Prompt JSON rendering now recursively uses the TypeScript-compatible numeric formatter for nested arrays and objects while preserving deterministic sorted object keys. Added nested 1.0e20 object/array regression coverage. JavaScript reference command, focused Swift test, and full Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 209 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: The 1.0e20 formatting fix still used Decimal(value) for ordinary finite numbers, exposing binary approximation tails unlike TypeScript/Bun String(number) for values such as 0.30000000000000004 and 1.2345678901234567.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Prompt rendering now starts from Swift's shortest round-tripping String(Double), expands positive exponents below 1.0e21, normalizes exponent zero padding, strips .0 from whole numbers, and covers representative TypeScript/Bun String(number) cases. Focused Swift test passed and full Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 208 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Numeric prompt rendering avoided the large-number Int64 trap but used compact JSON formatting, causing 1.0e20 to render as 1e+20 instead of TypeScript/Bun String(number) output 100000000000000000000.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Prompt rendering now formats finite numbers with TypeScript-like display rules, including decimal output for 1.0e20 and exponent normalization, while still avoiding unchecked Int64 conversion. The large-number regression now expects 100000000000000000000. JavaScript reference command, focused Swift test, and full Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 208 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Prompt rendering could crash the Swift process for integral JSON numbers outside Int64 bounds because formatTemplateValue converted integral Double values through unchecked Int64(value).
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Prompt rendering no longer uses unchecked Int64 conversion for numeric JSONValue values. Added regression coverage for 1.0e20. Focused Swift test passed and full Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 208 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Rendered prompt templates resolving to an empty string were replaced with step or workflow fallback prompt text, violating TASK-003 missing/null variable behavior.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: DeterministicWorkflowRunner now uses fallback prompt text only when no promptTemplate is configured, preserving configured templates that render empty. Added regression coverage for '{{ missing.path }}' with non-empty step description. Focused Swift test passed and full Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 207 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Swift execution built AdapterExecutionInput.promptText from step.description or workflow.description and never rendered AgentNodePayload prompt templates or applied WorkflowStepRef.promptVariant.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: DeterministicWorkflowRunner now applies prompt variants, renders composed session-start, prompt, and system templates with merged variables before adapter execution, and adds regression coverage asserting adapter input receives hydrated/rendered prompt text. Focused Swift test passed and full Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 206 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: TASK-003 required Swift prompt rendering fixtures, prompt asset loading contracts, escaped and missing variable behavior, output-envelope normalization coverage, and runtime-owned publication preservation before TASK-009.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added Swift prompt template asset fields and hydration, deterministic path and unreadable-file diagnostics, resolver integration, prompt rendering fixture tests, strict output-envelope and escaped-brace candidate extraction tests, and verified that runtime-owned publication remains outside backend adapters. Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 205 tests; TypeScript/Bun fallback checks passed.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Plan preserved official SDK backend strings but did not explicitly plan or defer official SDK Swift adapter behavior.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Expanded TASK-004 and verification/completion criteria to require official/openai-sdk and official/anthropic-sdk dispatch, request construction, API-key handling, retry/error normalization, output normalization, and no-live-credential tests; official/cursor-sdk remains explicitly deferred unless separately scoped.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Addon-only registry nodes were materialized with fabricated nodeFile paths even when authored workflow nodes declared only addon metadata.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Updated WorkflowNodeRef.nodeFile to be optional, preserved addon-only runtime nodes without synthesized node JSON files, and added fixture assertions for step10-git-commit and step11-git-push.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Swift workflow validation accepted unsafe nodeFile and stepFile values without the TypeScript workflow-relative path guard.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added workflow-relative path validation for nodeFile and stepFile, rejecting absolute paths and '.' / '..' segments before materialization, with RielaCoreTests regression coverage.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: The public validateAuthoredWorkflowJSON API bypassed raw nodeFile/stepFile safety checks for typed workflows.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Routed validateAuthoredWorkflowJSON through shared typed workflow-relative path validation before materialization and added direct typed API regression tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Typed workflow validation allowed unsafe synthesized nodeFile paths because validateAuthoredWorkflowJSON did not enforce node id and nodeFile/addon invariants before materialization.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added typed workflow semantic validation for workflowId, node ids, duplicate ids, required nodeFile/addon bindings, step references, and transition/fanout references before materialization, with direct typed API regression coverage.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: The real process runner waited for process termination before reading stdout/stderr and ignored AdapterExecutionContext.deadline, allowing verbose local agents to deadlock on full pipes or run without timeout cleanup.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Updated FoundationLocalAgentProcessRunner to drain stdout/stderr concurrently, pass AdapterExecutionContext.deadline through LocalAgentProcessRunning, terminate child processes on deadline, return AdapterExecutionError.timeout, and added AgentAdapterTests coverage for large output and deadline termination.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Deadline handling could still hang indefinitely because timeout completion waited for stdout/stderr EOF after process.terminate when a child retained pipe handles or the process ignored SIGTERM.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Made deadline expiry resume AdapterExecutionError.timeout independently of pipe EOF, close pipe handles during timeout cleanup, terminate the direct process, schedule SIGKILL escalation, and added AgentAdapterTests coverage for a TERM-resistant pipe-holding command.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Timeout cleanup signaled only the direct Process PID, allowing shell-wrapper descendants with inherited workspace access or stdout/stderr handles to survive after AdapterExecutionError.timeout.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Changed FoundationLocalAgentProcessRunner to launch with posix_spawn and POSIX_SPAWN_SETPGROUP, signal the process group on deadline with direct-PID fallback, schedule SIGKILL escalation, and added AgentAdapterTests coverage that verifies a spawned child process is terminated after timeout.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Swift migration progress reused phase 193 and changed the shared phase status from COMPLETED to IN_PROGRESS, conflicting with completed/manager-control-idempotency-atomic-claim.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Restored phase 193 to COMPLETED for completed/manager-control-idempotency-atomic-claim, assigned active/swift-native-migration to distinct phase 195, and added phase 195 with IN_PROGRESS status.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Local agent stdout was parsed as JSON whenever it contained braces even when input.node.output was absent, so free-form agent text could fail the step or become an unintended structured payload.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Changed LocalAgentCommandAdapter to parse provider stdout as an envelope only when the node declares an output contract; no-contract stdout now always becomes a text business payload. Added AgentAdapterTests coverage for JSON-looking and fenced no-contract text plus contracted envelope parsing.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Provider failure errors included raw stderr without credential redaction, allowing local CLI failures to publish API keys, tokens, or secret-like values.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added stderr redaction before AdapterExecutionError.providerError construction for secret-like assignments, sk-* tokens, bearer/token values, and sensitive environment values. Added AgentAdapterTests coverage for failing provider stderr with OPENAI_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, CURSOR_API_KEY, and bearer token material.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Nodes with an output contract could still succeed with plain text stdout because normalizeAgentOutput fell back to normalizeTextBusinessPayload when no JSON-looking text was present.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Removed the contracted-output text fallback so output-contracted local agents always parse stdout through parseJSONObjectCandidate and normalizeOutputContractEnvelope, returning invalidOutput for plain text. Added AgentAdapterTests coverage for output-contract plain text failure.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: spawnProcess did not explicitly close child-unused pipe descriptors, allowing stdin-consuming CLI agents to inherit a write end and potentially hang waiting for EOF.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Passed all pipe descriptors into spawnProcess and added child-side posix_spawn close actions for stdin writer, stdout reader, and stderr reader. Added AgentAdapterTests coverage using /bin/cat to verify stdin EOF is observed before the deadline.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Codex Swift target invoked codex exec --json but the shared local-process adapter parsed the whole stdout as one business JSON candidate, allowing Codex CLI JSONL/session event metadata to be accepted instead of the final assistant result.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added codex-agent stdout normalization before shared output-contract parsing: JSONL events are parsed, final assistant content is selected from assistant.snapshot or response_item/message payloads, and non-final metadata no longer becomes the payload. Added AgentAdapterTests coverage for contracted and text payload Codex JSONL streams.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: TASK-004 and the backend adapter module remained Not Started even though current implementation changed LocalAgentProcess and AgentAdapterTests for local-agent/Codex JSONL hardening.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Marked prompt/output-contract and backend-faithful agent adapter modules IN_PROGRESS, set TASK-003 and TASK-004 to In Progress in the plan and PROGRESS.json, checked off completed local CLI hardening and codex-agent JSONL criteria under TASK-004, and added latest progress log with TASK-002, TASK-003, and TASK-004 in progress.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Timeout SIGKILL escalation could signal a stale process id after the original child exited, risking termination of an unrelated reused pid or process group.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: LocalProcessHandle now tracks exited state, cancels scheduled kill work in markExited after waitpid reaps the child, no-ops signal attempts after process reap, and AgentAdapterTests adds deterministic coverage for canceling delayed SIGKILL after process reap.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: LocalProcessHandle.markExited canceled delayed SIGKILL whenever the direct child was reaped, leaving TERM-resistant descendants alive after timeout.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Split direct pid and process-group tracking. markExited(afterTimeout: true) clears only the direct pid and preserves processGroupId for scheduled SIGKILL; normal reaps still cancel delayed kill. Added handle-level SIGKILL preservation coverage and a Foundation runner test with a TERM-resistant child descendant.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: markExited(afterTimeout: true) preserved delayed SIGKILL without proving the process group still contained descendants, reopening stale process-group signaling risk when the group was already empty.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: markExited(afterTimeout: true) now probes kill(-processGroupId, 0) through processGroupIsLive before preserving scheduled SIGKILL. Empty process groups clear processGroupId and cancel delayed kill; live groups retain escalation. AgentAdapterTests covers both live and gone timeout-reap cases plus the TERM-resistant descendant runner path.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Delayed SIGKILL signaled the stored process group after an earlier liveness probe; descendants could exit before the delayed kill fired and make the process-group id stale.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: killScheduledProcessGroup now re-checks processGroupIsLive immediately before SIGKILL and clears stale scheduled process groups without signaling. AgentAdapterTests covers the race where markExited sees a live group but the delayed-kill probe sees it gone.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: High-risk Swift process-control changes and delayed-SIGKILL race fix lacked Swift test evidence because default swift lookup failed.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Located Xcode Swift at /Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer/Toolchains/XcodeDefault.xctoolchain/usr/bin/swift, ran Swift 6.3.2 with DEVELOPER_DIR and SDKROOT pointed at Xcode, and swift test passed 28 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Implemented the accepted TASK-004 official OpenAI and Anthropic SDK parity slice after Step 5 accepted the plan with no high or mid findings.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added provider-neutral official SDK DTOs and injected request executor infrastructure, registered official/openai-sdk and official/anthropic-sdk Swift adapter factories, kept official/cursor-sdk deferred, clamped retry delays, added default HTTP-backed production request executors, threaded exact API-key redaction through failures, and verified no-live-credential XCTest coverage with Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passing 44 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Self-review found RetryPolicy clamped maxAttempts but did not clamp negative retryDelay even though the accepted official SDK parity slice required retry delay clamping.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: RetryPolicy now clamps negative retryDelay to .zero and AdapterUtilitiesTests covers maxAttempts plus retryDelay clamping; later Step 7 adversarial fix raised swift test coverage to 42 passing tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Default official OpenAI and Anthropic dispatch factories resolved to adapters whose default executor was MissingOfficialSDKRequestExecutor, so real backend execution could not work without test-only injection even when API keys were present.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Replaced the missing default with URLSessionOfficialSDKRequestExecutor backed by injectable OfficialSDKHTTPTransporting. Default adapters now build OpenAI Responses and Anthropic Messages HTTP requests, decode JSON responses, redact non-2xx failures, and tests prove default DispatchingNodeAdapter factories execute through HTTP transports without injected request executors or live credentials.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Self-review found configured base URLs that already include shared path prefixes, such as Anthropic /v1, could duplicate path components and produce /v1/v1/messages.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: officialSDKEndpoint now appends only missing path components; the default Anthropic HTTP executor test uses https://anthropic.test/v1 and expects https://anthropic.test/v1/messages; swift test passed 42 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Official SDK failure redaction was pattern-based and did not redact exact configured API key values from injected environments or custom apiKeyEnv values, allowing provider or injected-executor errors that echoed arbitrary configured credentials to leak secrets in AdapterExecutionError.message.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Threaded resolved request.apiKey into official SDK failure normalization and redacts exact occurrences after shared redaction. Also redacts exact request.apiKey values in URLSessionOfficialSDKRequestExecutor before direct non-2xx provider errors are thrown. Added deterministic no-live tests for OpenAI HTTP non-2xx body, injected Anthropic executor error, and direct URLSession executor non-2xx error echoing custom configured secrets; swift test passed 45 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Self-review found URLSessionOfficialSDKRequestExecutor direct non-2xx errors should also redact exact request.apiKey values before throwing, not only rely on adapter-level normalization.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: URLSessionOfficialSDKRequestExecutor now redacts exact request.apiKey occurrences in non-2xx detail before throwing; testURLSessionOfficialSDKRequestExecutorRedactsDirectHTTPFailures covers direct executor use; swift test passed 45 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Implemented the accepted remaining TASK-004 local-agent command-builder and readiness parity slice after Step 5 accepted the plan with no high or mid findings.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added LocalAgentCommandBuilding and backend-owned Swift builders for codex-agent, claude-code-agent, and cursor-cli-agent; added deterministic Swift readiness/runtime-readiness APIs for available, unavailable, unknown, and not_checked states; added no-live XCTest coverage for exact argv/config/stdin/deadline behavior, policy_blocked preflight failures, readiness invalid/unknown results, and Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passing 51 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Self-review found the TASK-004 markdown status header still said In Progress while TASK-004 completion criteria, progress log, and impl-plans/PROGRESS.json recorded completion.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Corrected active plan status headers so TASK-002 and TASK-003 remain In Progress and TASK-004 is Completed, matching impl-plans/PROGRESS.json and Step 6 verification evidence.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Self-review found TASK-004H required injectable probe operations, but the first Step 6 implementation only exposed readiness report mapping APIs and tests built reports directly.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added AgentBackendProbeOptions plus CodexAgentReadinessOperations, ClaudeCodeAgentReadinessOperations, and CursorCLIAgentReadinessOperations protocols with async readiness API entry points. Added testReadinessAPIsUseInjectedProbeOperations with no-live mock operations; Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 51 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 review found default Codex, Claude, and Cursor adapter paths only ran auth/readiness preflight when callers injected checkAuthPreflight, so failed login, unavailable CLI, or unavailable model checks could surface as provider_error instead of accepted policy_blocked preflight behavior.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added default runner-backed preflight before local agent execution: Codex auth status, Claude --version plus auth status, and Cursor --version plus model check. Added no-live XCTest coverage for default Codex login failure, Claude unavailable CLI/auth failures, and Cursor unavailable CLI/auth/model failures; Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 54 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 review found TASK-001 was marked In Progress in the active plan while impl-plans/PROGRESS.json recorded TASK-001 as Completed.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Corrected TASK-001 status in impl-plans/completed/swift-native-migration.md to Completed.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Codex command construction sent the prompt on stdin and never appended the combined prompt as the final argv entry, while codex-agent builds codex exec --json ... <prompt>.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: CodexAgentCommandBuilder now appends supported image args, image separator when needed, and the combined system/user prompt as the final codex exec argv entry with empty stdin. Tests assert exact argv, prompt boundary, and CODEX_HOME propagation.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Cursor command construction omitted --print --output-format stream-json -- <prompt>, sent prompt on stdin, and added unsupported --stream-mode.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: CursorCLIAgentCommandBuilder now emits cursor-agent --print --output-format stream-json --model <model>, optional non-default --mode, supported image/additional args, --, final combined prompt, and empty stdin; unsupported stream-mode/effort CLI flags were removed from Swift Cursor command construction.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Cursor model preflight used cursor-agent --model <model> --check instead of the cursor-cli-agent model-availability probe shape.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Cursor default model preflight now runs cursor-agent --print --output-format text --model <model> -- Reply with exactly OK. Tests assert the exact no-live probe argv.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Default auth/readiness preflights did not propagate adapter environment passed to command builders, so configured PATH/auth homes could be absent during preflight.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: CodexAgentAdapter, ClaudeCodeAgentAdapter, and CursorCLIAgentAdapter now store the configured environment and merge it into default preflight configurations with RIELA_AGENT_BACKEND. Tests assert CODEX_HOME, CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR, and CURSOR_CONFIG_DIR reach preflight and main execution.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Codex default auth preflight used codex auth status while the codex-agent readiness reference uses codex login status.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: runCodexDefaultAuthPreflight now invokes [executableName, "login", "status"] and keeps policy_blocked mapping plus adapter environment propagation. AgentAdapterTests asserts the exact argv in Codex command-builder and default preflight failure coverage; Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 54 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Swift image/attachment command builders read only node.variables imagePaths or attachmentPaths while TypeScript parity resolves images from AdapterExecutionInput.mergedVariables and arguments and honors forwardImageAttachments=false.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added shared resolveAdapterImagePaths in RielaAdapters over mergedVariables and arguments, with nested image descriptor/source support, dedupe, depth limiting, and forwardImageAttachments=false. Codex and Cursor builders now use it for --image, Claude merges it into attachment behavior, and AgentAdapterTests covers mergedVariables, arguments, nested descriptors, dedupe, and disabled forwarding across Codex, Claude, and Cursor; Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 56 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: authPreflight:false was ignored when checkAuthPreflight was supplied, so injected preflight still ran for Codex, Claude, and Cursor local-agent execution.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Gated both injected and default preflight work behind authPreflight in all three adapters. When authPreflight is true, execution prefers checkAuthPreflight and otherwise runs the default preflight. Added no-live testAuthPreflightFalseSkipsInjectedPreflightForLocalAgents with throwing injected preflights for codex-agent, claude-code-agent, and cursor-cli-agent; Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 57 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Cursor command execution requested --output-format stream-json but returned raw JSONL stdout through LocalAgentCommandAdapter without Cursor-specific normalization, so final assistant/session.completed text could be missed before output-contract parsing.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added normalizeCursorStreamJSONStdout in CursorCLIAgentCommandBuilder to extract latest session.assistant_message displayText/rawText or fall back to session.completed.result before shared output normalization. Added no-live XCTest coverage for Cursor stream-json text payload extraction and output-contract envelope parsing; Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 59 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Default local-agent auth/readiness preflights reused AdapterExecutionContext.deadline and had no independent bounded timeout, so nil deadlines could hang and preflight timeouts could surface as timeout instead of policy_blocked auth/readiness failures.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added defaultAgentPreflightDeadline and agentPreflightErrorDetail helpers, applied 5-second Codex/Claude and 30-second Cursor default preflight bounds, mapped runner timeout/provider errors to redacted policy_blocked auth/CLI/model messages, and added no-live XCTest coverage for nil-deadline bounds plus timeout-to-policy_blocked mapping; Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 61 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Codex command construction only inserted the argv option terminator -- when image paths existed, so a flag-like prompt could be parsed as codex CLI options instead of the positional prompt.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: CodexAgentCommandBuilder now always appends -- before the final prompt argument after all supported flags/additional args. Added testCodexCommandBuilderTerminatesOptionsBeforeFlagLikePrompt with --model other prompt coverage; Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 62 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: External local-agent processes were spawned with POSIX_SPAWN_SETPGROUP but without a close-on-exec default for unrelated inherited file descriptors, allowing child agents to inherit parent runtime descriptors.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: FoundationLocalAgentProcessRunner now sets POSIX_SPAWN_CLOEXEC_DEFAULT while explicitly duping stdin/stdout/stderr; added testFoundationRunnerClosesUnrelatedInheritedFileDescriptors; Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 65 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Local-agent failure redaction did not include sensitive values supplied through LocalAgentProcessConfiguration.environment, so raw configured CODEX_HOME/CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR/CURSOR_CONFIG_DIR or token values could leak in provider_error or policy_blocked details.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added sensitiveAdapterEnvironmentValues and additionalSensitiveValues redaction plumbing through provider errors, compact readiness messages, agentPreflightErrorDetail, and Codex/Claude/Cursor default preflights; added configured environment redaction tests for provider_error and policy_blocked paths; Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 65 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Created focused implementation plan for accepted TASK-005 design scope.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added runtime session/message value types, store protocols, deterministic in-memory behavior, candidate-path normalization, output validation, publication boundary, verification commands, and legacy inbox/outbox exclusion criteria.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Parent plan issue-reference block still named the previous TASK-004 local-agent workflow session and issue title after the TASK-005 focused plan was created.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Updated the parent issue-reference block to name the current TASK-005 workflow session, issue title, and self-review node while preserving the prior TASK-004 session as historical context.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Focused TASK-005 plan was marked Ready while parent completed/swift-native-migration:TASK-002 and TASK-003 were still recorded as blockers.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Marked the focused TASK-005 plan Blocked, set phase 196 to BLOCKED, updated README status, and added an activation rule requiring parent TASK-002/TASK-003 completion or explicit stability acceptance before implementation starts.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Candidate-path work planned reader/validation behavior but omitted explicit runtime-owned provisioning, pre-attempt clearing, and post-attempt cleanup or ignore lifecycle.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added RuntimeCandidatePathStaging and RuntimeCandidatePathReservation planning, prepareCandidatePath/finalizeCandidatePath lifecycle expectations, checklist items, task deliverables, completion criteria, and rg verification commands.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: TASK-001 markdown status still said Not Started while PROGRESS.json marked it Blocked on parent TASK-002/TASK-003 dependencies.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Updated TASK-001 markdown status to Blocked so the focused plan, progress index, and activation rule agree before renewed Step 5 review.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: TASK-005 required Swift runtime-owned session/message APIs, candidate-path publication, output validation, and no-publication failure behavior while preserving TypeScript/Bun fallback.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Implemented deterministic in-memory runtime session, workflow message store, candidate-path staging/reader, output validation, and publication APIs with focused RielaCoreTests. Swift test passed 77 tests; TypeScript typecheck, Biome lint, and workflow validation passed.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 adversarial review requested runtime publication boundary hardening for exact candidate-path ownership and adapter/provider failure handling.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Runtime publication now rejects candidate-path submissions that do not match the exact reserved candidate path and exposes adapterFailure handling that marks the step failed without publishing messages. Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 79 tests; TypeScript typecheck, Biome lint, and workflow validation passed.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 review found unsafe candidate staging path components before recursive cleanup and shallow Swift output-contract validation versus packages/riela-core/src/json-schema.ts.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Runtime candidate staging now rejects unsafe path components and refuses outside-root finalization; Swift output validation now covers additionalProperties, enum, const, nested items/properties, numeric/string bounds, strict integer checks, and anyOf/oneOf/allOf. Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 82 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 review found Swift output-contract validation validated payload values directly but did not validate schema definitions like packages/riela-core/src/json-schema.ts, allowing unsupported or malformed contract keywords to be silently ignored.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: DefaultWorkflowOutputValidator now validates schema definitions before payload validation, rejects unsupported keywords and malformed type/properties/required/additionalProperties/items/enum/bounds/pattern/combinator definitions, and has malformed-schema regression tests. Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 83 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 adversarial review found candidate source precedence could bypass candidate-path reservation validation and publication did not finalize candidate-path staging after consumption.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Runtime publication now rejects ambiguous candidate sources, requires candidate-path requests to use the reserved candidate path when a reservation is present, and finalizes candidate-path staging after runtime consumption. Added tests for adapterOutput+candidatePath ambiguity and cleanup on success, validation failure, and append failure. Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 88 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 adversarial review found unsupported cross-workflow, resume-step, and fanout transition fields were silently converted into direct in-workflow messages, creating misleading successful publication.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Runtime publication now rejects unsupported transition semantics before accepted output or message append. Added tests for toWorkflowId, resumeStepId, and fanout transitions proving no acceptedOutput and no workflow messages. Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 89 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 review found prior WorkflowMessageRecord rows were not resolved into one structured adapter/executor input object and root output selection used transitions.isEmpty, allowing any terminal step to publish external workflow output.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added runtime-owned WorkflowResolvedMessageInput and DefaultWorkflowMessageInputResolver with deterministic ordering/filtering/merge tests and AdapterExecutionInput application. Added explicit publishesRootOutput metadata so terminal non-output steps do not publish rootOutput. Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 91 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 review found candidate-path staging could follow safe-named symlink components outside the staging root and message input resolution consumed created, failed, or superseded workflow message rows.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Candidate staging now validates existing path components before creation, validates the resolved staging directory after creation, and rejects symlink escapes without returning outside-root candidate paths. Runtime message append now stores direct messages as delivered, and DefaultWorkflowMessageInputResolver includes only delivered or consumed messages. Added symlink escape and lifecycle filtering regressions. Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 93 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 3 accepted TASK-006 design with no findings; no Step 5 feedback exists for this run.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Created focused TASK-006 implementation plan and linked parent plan, README, PROGRESS.json, task breakdown, and verification commands.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 review reported two TASK-006 mid findings: Swift package manifest metadata and validation parity gaps, and event validation parity gaps versus the TypeScript contracts.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added TS-compatible manifest metadata fields, dependency string decoding, required metadata/checksum/add-on safety validation, file-change and s3-repository event contracts, template reference validation, and output destination validation with deterministic XCTest regressions.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Implementation author self-review found nested package manifest DTOs for add-on capabilities, workflow metadata, integrity metadata, and signatures could still ignore unsupported keys.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added fail-closed unsupported-key decoding for those nested DTOs and deterministic decoder regressions.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 review reported incomplete add-on capability/capabilityGrant parity and dry-run input mapping that did not render TypeScript-compatible event templates.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added capability name, defaultPolicy, sensitive reason, duplicate, scope, and dependency capabilityGrant validation. Added event-input/template dry-run rendering with event, source, and binding roots, exact-reference object preservation, array traversal, and deterministic regressions. Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 115 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 review reported EventBindingContract omitted enabled and match rules, and hook event normalization omitted the full TypeScript HookEventName catalog.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added EventBindingContract enabled and match rules with eventType, conversationId, and pathPrefix dry-run matching. Expanded hook event normalization to the full known TypeScript HookEventName catalog and added deterministic regressions. Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 117 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Implementation author self-review found Swift synthesized Decodable would require enabled on event sources and bindings even though TypeScript event config treats enabled as optional.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added custom decoding defaults for EventSourceContract.enabled and EventBindingContract.enabled, plus deterministic decoding regression. Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 118 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 review found HookContext Codable decode required newly added non-optional fields, breaking pre-TASK-006 minimal JSON with only agentSessionId and optional agentBackend.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added custom HookContext decoding defaults for vendor, eventName, workingDirectory, and backendMetadata, plus deterministic regression. Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 119 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 review found EventBindingContract required workflowId instead of TypeScript workflowName, dry-run input mapping accepted unsupported merge fallback, and outputDestinations were omitted from runtime-variable evidence.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added workflowName and execution-mode binding contracts, typed event-input/template input mapping decoding, workflowName validation exceptions for supervisor-dispatch and schedule-registration, outputDestinations dry-run runtime variables, and deterministic regressions. Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 120 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 review found event dry-run runtime variables omitted humanInput and eventMailboxBridgePolicy, GraphQL schemaContract omitted DTO inspection fields and undefined ControlPlaneResult, and hook context resolution missed Riela env context plus invalid recording/capture validation.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added humanInput mirroring and eventMailboxBridgePolicy runtime variables, expanded schemaContract and schema assertions, added Riela hook context resolver/env validation, and deterministic regressions. Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 125 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 review found add-on manifest validation did not preserve TypeScript execution-artifact safety and EventBindingContract omitted authored mailboxBridge policy overrides.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Tightened add-on source/execution artifact path and descriptor validation, added authored EventMailboxBridgePolicy decoding and dry-run application, and added deterministic regressions. Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 129 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 review found EventSourceContract missed TypeScript webhook/S3 keys, EventContractValidator omitted mailboxBridge consumer compatibility rules, and GraphQL envelope parsing did not trim or normalize query, operationName, and variables like TypeScript.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added TypeScript-shaped webhook and nested S3 event source decode/encode support, mailboxBridge consumer mode validation, GraphQL query and operationName trimming plus variables null normalization, and deterministic regressions. Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 131 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 review found event route conflict validation ignored effective S3/default HTTP paths, webhook and S3 required-field validation was incomplete, and server context sanitization leaked ambient workflow execution keys.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added effective event HTTP path resolution for webhook and s3-repository sources, route conflict validation across explicit and default paths, required webhook path and S3 eventReceiver validation, ambient workflow environment stripping in ServerRequestContext, and deterministic regressions. Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 132 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 adversarial review found workflow package manifest validation ignored packageRoot and accepted missing workflow.json bundles, and event HTTP path resolution omitted chat-sdk webhook routes from conflict detection.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added non-destructive workflow.json file existence validation to FileWorkflowPackageManifestLoader.validate, added TypeScript-shaped chat-sdk webhook decode/encode and effective route conflict validation, and added deterministic regressions. Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 135 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 6 self-review confirmed chat-sdk webhook route parity and tightened missing webhook/path/auth validation before independent review.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added hasChatWebhook tracking, required chat-sdk webhook/path/auth diagnostics, updated dry-run chat fixtures to use valid webhook contracts, and re-ran targeted and full Swift tests. Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 135 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 adversarial review found HookParsing copied raw sensitive hook payloads into public HookContext.backendMetadata and server header normalization could trap on duplicate mixed-case headers.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Redacted parsed hook backendMetadata with the existing hook redaction policy, replaced header normalization with deterministic duplicate handling, and added focused regressions. Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 137 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 review found chat-sdk event validation accepted missing or unsupported providers and did not reject binding match.eventType values unsupported by the TypeScript chat-sdk provider capability set.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added Swift chat-sdk provider validation for the TypeScript provider set, added provider event-type capability validation for binding match.eventType, and added deterministic validation and dry-run regressions. Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 141 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 review found chat-sdk capability validation checked only binding match.eventType while dry-run matching also honors top-level EventBindingContract.eventType.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Applied the same provider capability validation to top-level chat-sdk binding eventType, and added deterministic validation and dry-run regressions for unsupported top-level chat-sdk event types. Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 143 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 adversarial review found FileWorkflowPackageManifestLoader accepted arbitrary URL schemes through Data(contentsOf:) and hook payload hashes used unsorted JSON encoder output.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added deterministic non-file URL rejection before manifest reads, canonicalized hook payload hash encoding with sorted JSON keys, and added focused regressions. Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 145 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 review found manifest decoding defaulted missing tags to [] without validation and normalizePackageRelativePath accepted raw .. segments such as a/.. and a/../b.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added decoded tag-field tracking for top-level and workflow metadata manifests, tag validation for missing and empty entries, fail-closed package-relative path normalization for any raw .. segment, and focused regressions. Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 146 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 6 self-review found explicit JSON null tags could still be accepted by decodeIfPresent despite comm-000071 requiring tags to be an array of strings.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Changed present tag fields to decode [String] directly, rejecting null, and added top-level plus workflow metadata null-tag regressions. Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 147 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 adversarial review found GraphQL schemaContract exposed continueSession without an input payload argument even though GraphQLContinueSessionRequest carries input.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added ContinueSessionInput with workflowId, sessionId, and input: JSONObject!, changed continueSession to accept the structured input object, and added schema regressions that reject the old no-input mutation signature. Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 147 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 adversarial review found allowedBuiltins could authorize untrusted package add-ons with built-in names and normalizePackageRelativePath did not reject Windows absolute paths.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Required trusted source.builtin metadata together with allowedBuiltins for built-in resolution, rejected Windows absolute paths in general package-relative path normalization, and added focused regressions. Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 149 tests.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 review found deterministic run adapter/scenario failures bypassed TASK-005 failed-step publication and accepted run options were not implemented or rejected.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Published deterministic failures via TASK-005 runtime publication, wired loop/timeout bounds, rejected unsupported persistence/concurrency options with usage exit behavior, and added core plus CLI regression tests. Full Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test passed 163 tests; Swift validate/inspect/run smokes, unsupported run-option usage smoke, Bun typecheck/lint, and TypeScript fallback validation passed.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 review found TASK-007 CLI parity gaps for validate/inspect remote flag rejection and parseable JSON run failure output.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Rejected unsupported remote flags for validate/inspect, added structured JSON run failure envelopes, and covered both with focused Swift CLI regressions. Xcode Swift 6.3.2 focused RielaCLITests passed 10 tests; full swift test passed 166 tests; Swift success/failure smokes, Bun typecheck/lint, and TypeScript fallback validation passed.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 review found TASK-007 deterministic output-contract retry parity missing for maxValidationAttempts and scenario response sequences.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added deterministic retry attempts through TASK-005 publication with real attempt numbers and no rejected-attempt messages, plus Swift regression coverage. Full swift test passed 167 tests and TypeScript/Bun fallback checks passed.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 adversarial review found TASK-007 sequential run truncated multiple publishable direct transitions to the first downstream step.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added fail-closed multiple-direct-transition handling with failed TASK-005 execution recording and no downstream messages, plus Swift regression coverage. Full swift test passed 168 tests and TypeScript/Bun fallback checks passed.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 review found workflow validate --output json returned resolver and validation failures only on stderr, leaving stdout non-JSON for machine clients.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: WorkflowValidateCommand now emits WorkflowValidationFailureResult JSON envelopes for load, invalid workflow, malformed node-patch, and preflight failures when JSON output is requested. Added Swift CLI regressions for missing workflow, invalid workflow diagnostics, and malformed node-patch failures. Focused RielaCLITests passed 14 tests; full swift test passed 172 tests; missing-workflow JSON smoke parsed with jq; git diff --check and jq empty impl-plans/PROGRESS.json passed.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 adversarial review found workflow inspect --output json still returned empty stdout with plain stderr on resolver or validation failure.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: WorkflowInspectCommand now emits WorkflowInspectionFailureResult JSON envelopes for inspect failures when JSON output is requested, including diagnostics for invalidWorkflow. Added Swift CLI regressions for missing workflow and invalid workflow diagnostics. Focused RielaCLITests passed 16 tests; full swift test passed 174 tests; missing-workflow inspect JSON smoke parsed with jq; git diff --check and jq empty impl-plans/PROGRESS.json passed.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 adversarial review found parser-stage workflow usage failures bypassed JSON failure rendering even when raw arguments requested --output json.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: RielaCLIApplication now detects workflow --output json in raw arguments and emits command-specific JSON failure envelopes for validate, inspect, and run parser CLIUsageError cases while preserving usage exit code 2 and empty stderr. Added Swift CLI regressions for unsupported run endpoint, validate unknown option, and inspect missing option value parser failures. Focused RielaCLITests passed 19 tests; full swift test passed 177 tests; built-executable parser JSON smokes passed with empty stderr; git diff --check and jq empty impl-plans/PROGRESS.json passed.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 review found Swift workflow inspect returned null callable data for codex-design-and-implement-review-loop because node input contracts and description-only output contracts were not preserved.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Swift AgentNodePayload now decodes node input contracts, and WorkflowInspectCommand now always derives callable stepId, role, input, and output from the manager or entry step payload. Added Swift CLI regression coverage and verified project inspect callable JSON with jq. Focused RielaCLITests passed 20 tests; full swift test passed 178 tests; git diff --check and jq empty impl-plans/PROGRESS.json passed.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 adversarial review found scoped workflow resolution allowed path traversal outside project/user workflow roots and executable validation marked addon-only nodes runnable although deterministic run fails without a payload.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Scoped workflow parsing and resolver containment now reject unsafe scoped names using the TypeScript/Bun safe workflow token rule before catalog reads. Deterministic executable preflight now reports addon-only nodes unsupported unless a payload exists. Added Swift CLI regressions for validate/inspect/run unsafe scoped names and addon-only validate/run consistency. Focused RielaCLITests passed 22 tests; full swift test passed 180 tests; built-executable traversal/addon smokes passed with parseable JSON and empty stderr; git diff --check and jq empty impl-plans/PROGRESS.json passed.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 review found scoped project/user workflow containment used standardized paths without resolving symlinks, allowing safe-name symlinks to escape .riela/workflows.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: FileSystemWorkflowBundleResolver now resolves symlinks for candidate and root directories before containment checks and reads workflow/node files only from contained resolved directories. Added Swift CLI regressions for validate/inspect/run symlink escapes. Focused RielaCLITests passed 23 tests; full swift test passed 181 tests; built-executable symlink smokes passed; git diff --check and jq empty impl-plans/PROGRESS.json passed.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 review found scoped workflow resolution still followed symlinked workflow.json and node payload files outside the project/user workflow root before validation or execution.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: FileSystemWorkflowBundleResolver now resolves workflow.json and every nodeFile payload target before Data(contentsOf:) and rejects resolved file targets outside the resolved scoped workflow directory for project/user scopes. Added Swift CLI regressions for symlinked workflow.json and node payload files across validate, inspect, and run. Focused RielaCLITests passed 25 tests; full swift test passed 183 tests; built-executable symlink file smokes passed; git diff --check and jq empty impl-plans/PROGRESS.json passed.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 adversarial review found Swift deterministic run did not evaluate existing transition label expressions such as !(needs_revision), causing mock runs to strand after successful branch steps.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Added a shared Swift WorkflowBranchEvaluator for bare labels, negation, parentheses, &&, ||, always/never, and payload fallback. RuntimePublication and DeterministicWorkflowRunner multiple-transition safety now use the same evaluator. Added deterministic runner and evaluator regressions plus a built-executable negated-transition smoke. Focused core tests passed 13 tests; full swift test passed 187 tests; git diff --check and jq empty impl-plans/PROGRESS.json passed.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 7 adversarial review found Swift ScenarioNodeAdapter advanced mock scenario sequences by simple per-step call count instead of the TypeScript executionIndex, maxValidationAttempts, and attempt formula, so repeated steps with validation retry budgets could consume unused retry slots.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: AdapterExecutionInput now carries executionIndex and output attempt context. DeterministicWorkflowRunner populates executionIndex per step execution and maxValidationAttempts/attempt per adapter call. ScenarioNodeAdapter computes sequence index with the TypeScript formula, and Swift regression coverage proves a repeated step skips an unused retry slot. Focused RielaCLITests plus deterministic runner and branch tests passed 39 tests; full swift test passed 188 tests; built-executable retry-slot smoke passed; git diff --check and jq empty impl-plans/PROGRESS.json passed.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 3 accepted TASK-008 design with no high or mid findings in supplied workflow input; no Step 5 feedback exists for this run.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Created focused TASK-008 implementation plan, linked parent plan, marked parent TASK-008 Ready, and added verification commands for Swift artifact naming, local-only archive readiness, TypeScript/Bun fallback, and no publishing side effects.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Focused TASK-008 plan status for TASK-008E implied implementation progress before predecessor tasks started.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: TASK-008E is now Not Started in the plan and PROGRESS.json; progress log records plan/progress alignment as completed setup, not active implementation.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 3 accepted TASK-002/TASK-003 design with no high or mid findings; issue requires TASK-002 Swift-capable verification evidence mirrored into the active plan and TASK-003 prompt/render/load/envelope gaps made actionable before TASK-009.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Marked TASK-002 completed with Xcode Swift 6.3.2 swift test evidence, expanded TASK-003 deliverables and completion criteria for prompt rendering fixtures, prompt asset loading contracts, escaped/missing variable behavior, output-envelope normalization, runtime-owned publication boundaries, and TypeScript/Bun fallback preservation.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 3 accepted TASK-009 design with no high or mid findings in supplied workflow input; issue requires final deterministic parity, security, archive smoke, gate-manifest evidence, and adversarial review handoff before Swift release packaging cutover.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Created focused TASK-009 implementation plan, linked parent plan, marked parent TASK-009 Ready, registered TASK-009 subtasks in PROGRESS.json, and kept all cutover gates blocked until concrete deterministic evidence exists.

addressedReviewFindings.finding: Step 5 accepted the TASK-009 implementation plan with no high or mid findings in supplied workflow input.
addressedReviewFindings.resolution: Implemented deterministic TASK-009 gate evidence, added Swift GraphQL manager-control DTO/mutation parity coverage, opened only passed non-review cutover gates, and left task009-adversarial-review blocked with allowsProductionCutover=false.

codexAgentReference: Preferred ../../codex-agent root unavailable; TASK-009 uses current Riela TypeScript/Bun runtime, adapters, package/event/GraphQL/hook contracts, and packaging scripts as the authoritative local reference. Adjacent ../codex-agent/package.json remains reference-only for package executable metadata and must not be copied.
dependencies: TASK-009 depends on completed TASK-003 prompt/envelope contracts, TASK-007 Swift CLI parity, and TASK-008 packaging readiness gates
packaging/homebrew/swift-cutover-gates.json remains blocked until deterministic evidence exists for each gate
issueReference: tacogips/riela; issue title: Execute Swift TASK-009 final parity security and cutover gate; workflowInput.issueBody TASK-009 final parity/security/cutover handoff; no matching GitHub issue URL or number supplied
parallelizableTasks: TASK-009A baseline evidence and TASK-009E security audit may run in parallel only after completed prerequisites are confirmed and write scopes stay disjoint.
TASK-009B parity hardening, TASK-009C archive smoke, TASK-009D gate manifest update, and TASK-009F review handoff are sequential because they share evidence artifacts and cutover manifest state.
residualRisks: Production Homebrew remains on TypeScript/Bun until a dedicated release cutover changes the formula source; allowsProductionCutover remains false in packaging/homebrew/swift-cutover-gates.json.
Default swift lookup may still require explicit DEVELOPER_DIR and SDKROOT override.
Preferred local codex-agent reference root ../../codex-agent is unavailable; TASK-009 used current Riela TypeScript/Bun behavior as the authoritative local reference.
official/cursor-sdk remains explicitly deferred unless separately scoped and reviewed.
